mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis, the discount rate on equities declines faster than earnings expectations adjust, so S&P 500 prices should rise over the next 20 trading days. The signal is valid only when inflation is not accelerating at the same time.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with stable inflation
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in most instances where FEDFUNDS has declined over the prior 3 months and CPIAUCSL is not rising faster than its recent trend, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above 4.2% indicates labor-market softening that reduces earnings growth expectations and raises recession odds, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with UNRATE above 4.2% produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal loses predictive power after unemployment has already been elevated for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A joint rise in PAYEMS and RSAFS signals broadening household income and retail demand, which should support earnings revisions and push S&P 500 returns higher over the next month. The effect requires both labor income and sales activity to improve together.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-plus-RSAFS improvement does not outperform either series alone in walk-forward tests, or if SPX 20-day returns are not positive after joint upswings in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL indicates supply-side disinflation and better corporate margin conditions, which should lift equities over the next 60 trading days as input-cost pressure eases.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising INDPRO and subdued CPIAUCSL do not generate above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not stronger than either standalone proxy across rolling out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, tighter policy raises the equity discount rate and compresses valuation multiples, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days. This effect is strongest when CPIAUCSL is still elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if high-policy-rate episodes above 4.5% do not correspond to negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal reverses sign after controlling for CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST after prior strength signals weakening housing demand and a broader slowdown in cyclical activity, which should weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through lower growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% from its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST contractions of more than 8% from the 6-month average are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the effect disappears in non-housing-led slowdowns.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE indicates a genuine labor-market deterioration, which should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days because investors reprice recession risk immediately.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS declines and UNRATE increases do not outperform either series alone at the 5-day horizon, or if triggered instances produce positive SPX returns more often than not.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery, accelerating GDP growth from GDPC1 combined with rising RSAFS should support earnings breadth and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as both output and spending re-accelerate.
failure_condition: Reject if joint upswings in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to generate positive 60-day SPX returns in walk-forward tests, or if the interaction becomes negative when growth is already above trend.
